purpose: Deterministic transformation of source code into structured analytical representations for downstream reasoning systems.

ingestion.definition: Convert raw source code into parsed, traversable structures.
rules: Must not perform semantic interpretation beyond structural parsing
Must not depend on persistence layer
Must operate on file input only
Must be deterministic given same input

representation.definition: Transform AST and raw code into normalized structural entities.
rules: Must extract functions, classes, imports, and contracts consistently
Must not perform global analysis or cross-file reasoning
Must not embed storage assumptions
Must normalize outputs into stable schemas

analysis.definition: Derive higher-order structural signals from representations.
rules: May aggregate across files
May infer architecture-level patterns
Must not mutate source representations
Must produce explainable, structured outputs

indexing.definition: Optional layer for enabling retrieval and similarity search.
rules: Must be fully optional and non-required for system execution
Must not be required by ingestion or analysis layers
May include embeddings or external storage integration
Must remain replaceable without affecting core pipeline

orchestration.definition: Controls execution flow across all other domains.
rules: Must not implement domain logic
Must only coordinate calls between modules
Must remain stateless or minimally stateful
Must not embed analysis heuristics

core_invariants: Downward dependency only: ingestion → representation → analysis → orchestration
All outputs must be serializable structured data
System must remain functional without indexing layer
AST parsing is foundational but not interpretive
All analysis must be reproducible from source input

rules: All tools must emit structured machine-readable output
Narrative or interpretive text is optional and non-authoritative
Outputs must not assume persistence or external storage
Outputs must be self-contained

forbidden: reverse dependency (higher layer importing lower abstraction assumptions)

stability_principle.definition: The system must remain stable under arbitrary evolution of input codebases.
constraints: No tool may require redesign when project structure changes
All logic must be based on semantics of code, not file layout assumptions
All heuristics must be replaceable without schema breakage
